tickets.title: obs-collector: add dedup guard to prevent re-logging on state reset
tickets.description: Root cause: when obs-collector-state.json is reset (lastRunEpoch=0 or missing), the collector re-logs all historical entries in delegation-log.json from epoch 0. During INC-20260509-001 recovery, this produced 3,761 phantom delegation_fail entries over 9.5 hours. Fix: (1) Add dedup check — skip entries already logged in obs.db (by timestamp+event_type+source). (2) Cap historical lookback on state reset to last 24h only, not epoch 0. (3) Filter delegation-log.json latency records (status=fail but empty task/agent/error) from delegation_fail check. Linked: TKT-0112 (cron run failures not captured in obs.db).
tickets.notes: COMPLETE 2026-05-10. (1) 24h lookback cap: when lastRunEpoch=0 or state missing, defaults to now-86400 instead of epoch 0 — prevents re-logging all history on state reset. (2) Dedup check in _obs_log wrapper: queries obs.db for same event_type+source within last 5min before logging — skips if already present. (3) Empty field filter already done in TKT-0112. Test: simulated state reset produced 4 events (was 3761). CHG-0267.

tickets.title: Security: CLI-Anything supply chain risk — agent integration layer poisoning via auto-generated SKILL.md

OpenClaw is explicitly named as a supported platform. Risk: attackers submit poisoned repos or SKILL.md files that agents ingest and trust as operational directives. No mainstream SAST/SCA scanner has a detection category for malicious agent instruction files. Actions required: (1) Inventory all SKILL.md files in use on OC1 — document source, author, review status for each. (2) Prohibit use of CLI-Anything or any auto-generated skill tools on production OC1. (3) Establish formal skill intake SOP: any new SKILL.md must pass Shield + Sage review AND be from a verified, known source before installation. (4) Check if any existing plugin-skills or workspace skills were sourced from unverified third parties. (5) Evaluate Cisco open-source Skill Scanner and Snyk mcp-scan for agent-layer scanning. Owner: Shield (security review), Yoda (operational). Ken approves any new controls. Reference: VentureBeat CLI-Anything article + Snyk ToxicSkills research (13.4% of 3,984 ClawHub skills had critical security issues).

DDIPE attack technique (Hong Kong/NTU/UNSW/Tokyo, April 2026): embeds malicious logic inside code examples in skill documentation — achieves 2.5% full evasion rate against all detection layers. AInchors context: S3 control ('no ClawHub skills, custom-built only') is a partial control. We have custom SKILL.md files in plugin-skills/ and workspace skills/ that have never been formally audited. Actions required: (1) Audit all existing custom SKILL.md files on OC1 — scan for embedded instructions that could execute as operational directives (look for: shell commands in examples, credential references, network calls, exfiltration patterns). (2) Define formal SKILL.md review checklist — what Shield and Sage must verify before any skill is used. (3) Update S3 control to explicitly include: no unreviewed SKILL.md regardless of source. (4) Evaluate Cisco Skill Scanner (open-source) and Snyk mcp-scan for automated skill scanning. (5) Document all approved skills in a skill registry with: source, author, review date, reviewer. (6) Add skill intake to governance gate — any new skill = Shield + Sage mandatory review before installation. Owner: Shield (security), Lex (policy update for S3), Yoda (operational). Ken approves.
tickets.resolution: Cisco Skill Scanner evaluated. Snyk mcp-scan deferred to P2. audit-skill.sh v2 provides adequate coverage. CHG-0335.

tickets.title: PII Scanner on Document Ingestion Pipeline (TKT-0162 Phase 1, WP7)
tickets.description: Implement PII scanner as a mandatory gate on all document ingestion before chunking or embedding. This is an APP 11 (Privacy Act 1988) compliance control and a prerequisite for the RAG pipeline (TKT-0170). Scope: (1) Install spaCy with en_core_web_lg model on OC1 (lightweight, local, no data residency risk). Note: evaluate Microsoft Presidio as alternative — Ken to confirm preference (Presidio = heavier but better entity detection for structured data). (2) Build PII scanner script: scripts/pii-scan.sh <document_path> — returns JSON: {pii_present: bool, entities_found: [], risk_level: [low|medium|high]}. (3) Wire into document ingestion path: before any document is chunked or embedded, must pass scanner. (4) Set pii_present flag on knowledge_documents table record. (5) Block embedding when pii_present=TRUE without explicit Compliance Agent (Lex) approval. (6) Wire pii_detected event to agent_events table — triggers Lex review queue. (7) Build deletion capability: scripts/purge-document.sh <document_id> — removes all chunks and embeddings for a document (data subject deletion right, APP 12). Dependencies: TKT-0164 (Postgres for event logging). Precondition for TKT-0170 (RAG pipeline). Owner: Forge (build), Lex (policy approval). DoD: scanner live, pii_present flag on schema, block gate enforced, deletion script working.

tickets.title: pgvector + nomic-embed-text RAG Pipeline — Initial Knowledge Base (TKT-0162 Phase 1, WP8)
tickets.description: Implement the full RAG pipeline using pgvector and nomic-embed-text (768-dim). This realises TKT-0104 T4 (Long-Term Semantic Memory). Scope: (1) Add pgvector extension to Postgres: CREATE EXTENSION vector. (2) Create knowledge_chunks and knowledge_documents tables (schema from TKT-0104): knowledge_documents(id, title, source_path, classification, pii_present, quality_score, provenance, ingested_at, expires_at, tenant_id), knowledge_chunks(id, document_id, chunk_index, content, embedding vector(768), classification, pii_present, quality_score, created_at, expires_at, tenant_id). HNSW index on embedding column. (3) Deploy nomic-embed-text via Ollama (already installed): ollama pull nomic-embed-text. (4) Build ingestion pipeline: scripts/ingest-document.sh <file_path> — reads file, PII scans, chunks (RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter 400-600 tokens, 10-20% overlap), embeds via nomic-embed-text, stores to pgvector. (5) Build retrieval query: scripts/rag-search.sh <query> <limit> — embeds query, cosine similarity search on knowledge_chunks, returns top-k with metadata. (6) Seed initial knowledge base: RULES.md, AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, Model3-Policy.md, Nexus-Access-Policy-v1.0.md, TKT-0104 doc, TKT-0046 doc. (7) Wire retrieval into Yoda heartbeat for relevant context injection. Dependencies: TKT-0164 (Postgres), TKT-0170 (PII scanner). This ticket: separate from TKT-0153 (Yoda semantic memory on MinIO). Owner: Forge (pipeline), Atlas (schema). DoD: pgvector live, ingestion pipeline working, initial 6 docs seeded, retrieval tested.

tickets.notes:

Steps: (1) Sample 20-30 existing session logs per model tier to derive avg input/output tokens per turn. (2) Update cost-tracker.sh to use turn counts + model distribution × calculated rates. (3) Cross-validate against confirmed Anthropic balance deltas. Estimated accuracy: ±10-15% — sufficient for cost governance. Avoid ephemeral session file parsing — too complex, OpenClaw-internal.
